title: RestoPlatform
summary: Multi-tenant SaaS platform for independent restaurants — QR ordering, pay-at-table, reservations, delivery and analytics, one site per restaurant, zero commission.

caseStudy.sector: SaaS · Hospitality — multi-tenant, European data sovereignty
caseStudy.role: Sole architect-developer, with a multi-agent AI orchestration
caseStudy.timeline: Designed and built in a few months · 6 source projects, 150 test files
caseStudy.challenge: An independent restaurant owner today juggles a dozen tools — menu, reservations, QR ordering, payment, delivery, analytics — and hands a commission to aggregators on every meal. RestoPlatform brings it all into one platform, one site per restaurant, with zero commission.
caseStudy.approach: A multi-tenant SaaS platform in Clean Architecture: a real-time REST API, one public site per restaurant (Blazor SSR, crawlable) and an operations back-office (Blazor Server). All data in one shared PostgreSQL database, isolated per restaurant through defense-in-depth (application filter + PostgreSQL Row-Level Security). Hosted 100% in Europe on Clever Cloud.
caseStudy.outcome: A complete, tested, production-ready platform — pay-at-table ordering, menu, reservations, in-house delivery, loyalty, analytics and a staff dashboard — with the first restaurant client going live. A zero-commission model (flat subscription), GDPR-compliant and sovereign by design, backed by around 17,000 lines of code and 18,500 lines of tests.
caseStudy.diagramAlt: RestoPlatform Clean Architecture: REST API + SignalR, a Blazor SSR showcase site, a Blazor Server dashboard, a multi-tenant application layer, EF Core 10 / PostgreSQL infrastructure with Row-Level Security on Clever Cloud

decisions.title: Multi-tenant on a shared DB + Row-Level Security
decisions.detail: All restaurants share one PostgreSQL database, but every row is isolated by defense-in-depth: application filter, EF global filter, then PostgreSQL's native Row-Level Security (non-owner role, tenant set per transaction). A cross-tenant access returns 404, never 403 — no existence leak. Every entity passes a cross-tenant denial test before it ships.

decisions.title: European sovereign hosting (Clever Cloud)
decisions.detail: No hyperscaler: everything runs on Clever Cloud (French PaaS, SecNumCloud/HDS certified). PostgreSQL is a first-class managed database there, the cost floor stays low for a flat-subscription model, and data never leaves Europe. The boundaries (object storage, backplane, observability) stay swappable interfaces.

decisions.title: Zero commission: flat subscription
decisions.detail: The platform never takes a cut of the meal; Stripe and delivery fees are passed through. The tool is aligned with the owner's interest, not with margin extraction — the structural difference versus the aggregators.

decisions.title: Allergens can never be hidden
decisions.detail: As long as a dish is orderable, its allergen warning stays visible — impossible to hide, flag away or theme invisible. On a food product, that's a safety rule built into the design, not a display option.

decisions.title: Public render is SSR (never WASM) for SEO
decisions.detail: Every restaurant site is server-rendered: crawlable, with structured data (Restaurant, Menu) and hreflang automatic. Ordering interactivity uses small JavaScript islands and real-time — no per-guest Blazor circuit, so no heavy server-side state.

decisions.title: Stripe Connect: the restaurant is the merchant
decisions.detail: Each restaurant keeps its own Stripe account, payouts and disputes (direct KYC). Card data never touches us (Stripe Elements/Checkout only), which puts compliance at the lightest PCI tier (SAQ-A); SCA/3DS2 is handled by Stripe.

sections.title: Multi-tenancy & isolation
body: One shared PostgreSQL database, but four layers of defense: tenant context resolved server-side (never from client input), a filter in the repositories, an EF global filter + stamp on SaveChanges, and forced PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (non-owner application role, tenant set via SET LOCAL).
The rule is enforced by tests: every restaurant-owned entity must pass a cross-tenant denial test, run against a real PostgreSQL (Testcontainers), before any merge.

sections.title: Payment & compliance
body: Stripe Connect (the restaurant is the merchant, PCI SAQ-A), multi-country VAT applied per channel (on-site / takeaway / delivery), and informational multi-currency — the euro is the only charged currency, the rest are display-only via cached exchange rates.
GDPR by design: EU data residency, a separate append-only audit database, data-subject rights (access, export, erasure) and pseudonymisation on erasure.

sections.title: Real-time & async
body: SignalR for real-time (kitchen pass status, waiter calls) and Web Push for the waiter app. No broker in V0: reliability comes from an EF outbox and hosted services, idempotent on the event id.
Scheduled tasks run on every instance but are serialised by a PostgreSQL lock (pg_advisory_lock); the real-time backplane uses Clever Cloud's managed Redis.

sections.title: Quality & continuous integration
body: xUnit + NSubstitute for tests, Testcontainers PostgreSQL for integration (cross-tenant isolation, migrations replayed on a real database). Around 18,500 lines of tests for 17,000 lines of code.
GitHub Actions CI: a green build and tests act as the merge gate on develop (auto-merge on success); the main branch stays under human review.

title: DPELib
summary: A platform connecting the five audiences of France's energy-performance diagnostic — anonymous by default, secure payment, GDPR/CNIL/PSD2 compliant.

caseStudy.sector: PropTech · RegTech — Energy Performance Diagnostic (French regulation)
caseStudy.role: Sole architect-developer, with AI agents (Claude Code) to speed up delivery
caseStudy.challenge: Connect five audiences that don't naturally trust each other — homeowners, certified diagnosticians, notaries, estate agencies and building professionals — on a single platform. All of it with anonymity by default, secure payment, and a tight regulatory stack: GDPR, CNIL, PSD2/SCA and the official DPE calculation method.
caseStudy.approach: A Clean Architecture platform with four front-ends (REST API, mobile PWA for consumers, a four-role pro dashboard, and an SEO-first SSR showcase) on top of 153 business use cases. Identity is only revealed once an intervention is confirmed; payment is pre-authorized then captured only on confirmation; the public diagnostician registry is imported nightly from data.gouv.fr.
caseStudy.outcome: A genuine software trusted-third-party: the client books and pays securely (3DS2 pre-authorization), the diagnostician stays anonymous until confirmation, and money is only captured at the scheduled intervention — otherwise funds are released and a one-click re-booking is offered. GDPR, CNIL, PSD2 and DPE 3CL compliant, deployed serverless with zero stored secrets.
caseStudy.diagramAlt: DPELib Clean Architecture: 4 presentation apps, 153 application use cases, EF Core 10 / SQL Server infrastructure with a separate audit database

decisions.title: Anonymous by default, conditional reveal
decisions.detail: A diagnostician's identity is only disclosed once an intervention is confirmed; before that, phone numbers are masked via a Twilio proxy. On a matchmaking platform, trust is earned step by step — it isn't presumed.

decisions.title: Pre-authorized payment, deferred capture
decisions.detail: Funds are held at booking (3DS2/SCA) but only captured when the pro confirms. On silence: automatic reminders at D+2 and D+5, then expiry at D+7 with funds released. The client is never charged for an appointment that doesn't happen.

decisions.title: "Shadow" accounts from public data
decisions.detail: The official registry (data.gouv.fr) is imported nightly and de-duplicated; each diagnostician exists as an account-less profile, created only when a client reaches out (pre-filled magic link). The platform is useful from day one, without waiting for sign-ups.

decisions.title: Separate audit DB + systematic soft-delete
decisions.detail: Consents and audit live in a separate database, never in the business transaction; no physical deletion. The right to erasure (< 30 days) and CNIL traceability are guaranteed by the architecture, not by code discipline.

decisions.title: Dependency injection validated at build
decisions.detail: A test resolves every use case in the runtime: an unregistered dependency fails the build (ValidateOnBuild). Across 153 use cases, that's what makes adding features safe rather than nerve-racking.

decisions.title: Serverless deploy with zero stored secrets
decisions.detail: Azure Container Apps driven by azd, CI auth via OIDC federated credentials (no long-lived secrets), secrets in Key Vault. The pipeline is replayable, auditable, and leaves no keys lying around.

sections.title: From slot to payment
body: The client picks a slot, then Stripe pre-authorizes the amount (€80 + €30/item) with strong 3DS2/SCA authentication. The professional is notified by email and SMS.

If they confirm, funds are captured, identity is revealed and the intervention is scheduled. If they stay silent, reminders go out automatically, then the hold expires at D+7: funds are released and a one-click re-booking is offered to the client.

sections.title: Integrations
body: Six external systems: Stripe (PSD2 payment, idempotent webhooks), data.gouv.fr (daily CSV import via CRON + Polly retry), YOLO/ONNX Runtime (embedded ML inference), BDNB (building enrichment with cache), Twilio (number masking) and Gravatar.
Each integration is isolated behind an interface — testable, replaceable, and never exposed to the rest of the domain.

sections.title: DevOps & CI/CD
body: Full GitHub Actions pipeline: .NET 10 build, xUnit tests, coverage gate, Bicep template validation, azd deployment and post-release smoke tests on /alive and /health.
Infrastructure-as-Code in Bicep (12 files), Azure Container Apps as serverless runtime, secrets in Key Vault, and .NET Aspire locally to orchestrate SQL Server, Redis and storage.

sections.title: Regulatory compliance
body: GDPR: granular consents, a consent log in a separate database, right to erasure within 30 days. CNIL: blocking consent banner before any non-essential tracker.
PSD2/SCA on payment, DPE calculation compliant with the current 3CL method, and read-only alerts on rental bans (energy-sieve F/G properties).

title: Brasa Geneva
summary: Full platform for a Geneva cigar club — age-gated public showcase, member area, and a secure dashboard that runs the whole business: catalogue, subscriptions, payments and art gallery.
imageAlt: Screenshot of the Brasa Geneva platform

caseStudy.sector: Luxury · regulated e-commerce — premium cigars, artisan humidors and an art gallery for a private Geneva club
caseStudy.role: Sole architect-developer running a multi-agent AI orchestration (14-persona council + TDD-first delivery loop)
caseStudy.challenge: A private Geneva club wanted a single platform to sell premium cigars, artisan humidors and an art gallery — at once a public showcase, a member area and a back-office. All within one of the strictest legal frames there is: tobacco advertising, a ban on cross-border sales, mandatory age verification, GDPR and strong payment authentication.
caseStudy.approach: A single Clean Architecture solution serving three front-ends (age-gated WASM showcase, member area, Blazor Server back-office) and two user types, with the legal guardrails built into the design rather than bolted on afterwards. 100% Swiss hosting (Infomaniak), multi-channel payment, and an isolated audit layer for compliance.
caseStudy.outcome: A luxury sales platform compliant by design: cigar purchase blocked outside Switzerland, an unavoidable age-gate, data traced in a separate audit database. Delivered in ~4.5 months through orchestrated AI, with test effort exceeding production effort (2.2 : 1 ratio).
caseStudy.diagramAlt: Brasa Geneva Clean Architecture: 3 presentation apps, 99 application use cases, EF Core 10 / PostgreSQL infrastructure with a separate audit database

decisions.title: Legal guardrails by design
decisions.detail: Cigar sales are blocked outside Switzerland and the age-gate is unavoidable, both coded as domain rules — impossible to bypass via a URL or a UI state. Here, a bypass isn't a bug: it's a legal risk.

decisions.title: Physically separate audit DB
decisions.detail: Entity changes are traced in an append-only database, never in the same transaction as business data. GDPR compliance doesn't depend on the goodwill of application code — it's structural.

decisions.title: Sovereign hosting (Infomaniak, Switzerland)
decisions.detail: No managed Azure or AWS service: data sovereignty matches the Geneva luxury positioning, and the Kubernetes (Helm) deployment stays under full control end to end.

decisions.title: The back-office talks to the API, never the database
decisions.detail: The Dashboard (Blazor Server) goes through the API via a typed HttpClient: one single door to the data, so one single surface to secure and audit.

decisions.title: Embedded CRON over a message bus
decisions.detail: Background tasks (tenure badges, reorder reminders, Stripe retention) run on an embedded scheduler. The RabbitMQ dependency is scoped but not activated until a transactional need justifies it — no complexity paid up front.

decisions.title: Tests first (2.2 : 1 ratio)
decisions.detail: A test guarantees every use case has its own test class: impossible to add an uncovered business rule without breaking the build. On a regulated domain, quality is what protects the client.

sections.title: Multi-channel payment
body: Three payment methods, one funnel: card via Stripe (payment links, idempotent webhooks, controlled event retention), bank transfer and crypto.
Legal guardrail built into the funnel: cigars can only be purchased in Switzerland; outside CH, the journey switches to a quote or Club-only access.

sections.title: DevOps & sovereign hosting
body: The whole platform is published on Infomaniak Kubernetes. Infrastructure-as-Code in Helm (~25 templates) covering deployments, migration and admin-bootstrap jobs, HPA, PDB and NetworkPolicy.
.NET Aspire orchestrates the local environment; the GitHub Actions CI requires a green build and tests before any commit.

sections.title: Regulatory compliance
body: Tobacco advertising (regulated product copy, validated PR posture), cross-border sales blocked, an unavoidable age-gate.
GDPR: separate audit DB, systematic soft-delete, no personal data in clear text in the logs. The art gallery is protected by badge-gating and image protection.

sections.title: i18n & "light luxury" design
body: Seven locales handled in .resx files (FR/EN + de-CH, it-CH, es-ES, hu, bg) — every visible string must be translated.
A parchment / ink / ember aesthetic held by multi-persona quality control: Italiana + Cormorant Garamond + Jost type, slow animations, zero glass-morphism.

title: K-RO SQUARE
summary: Showcase and offer-presentation site for a French brand — pared-back art direction, clear staging of the identity.
